title: 2026 Jeep Wrangler — Feature Taxonomy (curated)
provenance_note: Feature identity, trim standard-vs-option status, and option codes/prices are read from config/order_guide.json (order_guide_2026, reissued 2025-08-06). The SELECTION of which features to include, the category assignment, and any equipment not line-itemed on the guide's non-exhaustive content pages (skid plates, crawl control) are curator judgment — hence the '+ curator' source. Where the guide's Rubicon/Sahara 'content' page conflicts with a separately-priced option (steel bumpers = option AD3), the taxonomy follows the priced option and notes the reconciliation. Community mod-target framing draws on discovery/report.md §3.2.
trims_note: Canonical trim names valid in every feature's standard_on. These match the trim names in Data_Trims (config/order_guide.json) except the fleet-only right-hand-drive Sport RHD, which is excluded from the taxonomy. Body style (JL 2-door vs JLU 4-door) is NOT a distinguishing dimension for any standard feature below — the off-road hardware is identical across bodies for a given trim — so plain trim names are used; body-specific facts (Sahara is 4-door only; Xtreme 35 content differs by body) live in per-feature notes.

features.name: Rear Locker
features.notes: Electronic locking rear differential (Tru-Lok). Willys via DSH E-Locker Rear Axle; Rubicon pairs it with a front locker. Not factory-optionable on Sport / Sport S / Sahara — a common regear+locker aftermarket target for those trims.

features.name: Front Locker
features.notes: Electronic locking front differential (Tru-Lok), paired with the rear on Rubicon. Not offered on any other trim from the factory.

features.name: Rock-Trac 4:1 Transfer Case
features.notes: Heavy-duty part-time 2-speed transfer case with a 4:1 low-range crawl ratio. Rubicon-exclusive; the single biggest crawl-ratio differentiator versus the 2.72:1 Command-Trac on Sport/Willys.

features.name: Selec-Trac Full-Time 2-Speed Transfer Case
features.notes: Full-time 4WD transfer case, usable on dry pavement, unique to Sahara (which is 4-door only). Sport / Sport S / Willys use part-time Command-Trac; Rubicon uses Rock-Trac.

features.name: Command-Trac Part-Time Transfer Case (2.72:1)
features.notes: Base part-time 2-speed transfer case, 2.72:1 low range. The reference point the Rubicon (4:1 Rock-Trac) and Sahara (full-time Selec-Trac) improve on.

features.name: 4.10 Axle Ratio
features.notes: Numerically higher (4.10) gearing for crawl and larger-tire drivability. Willys via DMF; standard on Rubicon. Sport / Sport S ship taller highway gears; a 4.10/4.56/4.88 regear is a common mod when fitting 35s. The factory 4.88 (DMS) is manual-only on Rubicon.

features.name: Heavy-Duty Wide Axles (Dana M210/M220)
features.notes: Wide-track heavy-duty front (Dana M210) and rear (Dana M220) axles. Willys and Rubicon share the wide axles; Rubicon adds heavier diffs and, with the factory 35s package, a full-floating rear (2-door). Sport / Sport S / Sahara use lighter axles.

features.name: Off-Road+ Drive Mode
features.notes: Off-Road+ (XG7) throttle and traction tuning for sand and rock. Standard on Willys and Rubicon.

features.name: Selec-Speed Crawl Control
features.notes: Low-speed off-road crawl control (hill-ascent/descent). Standard Rubicon equipment; not line-itemed on the guide's content page, so curator-sourced from Jeep trim spec.

features.name: Electronic Front Sway Bar Disconnect
features.notes: Electronically disconnecting front sway bar for wheel articulation. Rubicon-only from the factory and not optionable elsewhere — one of the most common aftermarket adds (manual or electronic) on Sport / Willys / Sahara builds.

features.name: Willys Off-Road Suspension
features.notes: Willys-specific tuned suspension (SDV) with a mild lift over Sport. Not a full Rubicon-height setup.

features.name: 35-Inch Tire Suspension (Factory Lift)
features.notes: Factory suspension geometry to clear 35s (SDG). Standard on Rubicon X 2.0T / V6-auto configs (22Y/24Y); available via the Xtreme 35 package (AGB) on Sport S / Willys / Rubicon. Package content differs by body style: the 2-door adds a full-time Rock-Trac + full-floating M220 rear + 5,650 lb GVWR; the 4-door adds performance brakes + a 6,250 lb GVWR (see order_guide_notes.md §4). Ambiguity: the guide labels only AGB “Xtreme 35”; the community “Xtreme Recon” 35 package maps to this entry.

features.name: 33-Inch Mud-Terrain Tires
features.notes: Roughly 33-inch (LT285/70R17) mud-terrain tires standard. Sport / Sport S run ~32-inch all-terrains; Sahara ~32-inch.

features.name: 35-Inch Tires (Factory)
features.notes: Factory 35-inch tires. Standard on Rubicon X 2.0T / V6-auto (22Y/24Y); optional via the Xtreme 35 package (AGB, $4,895 MSRP) on Sport S / Willys / Rubicon, which requires the 8-speed automatic (EC1/DFT). A frequent aftermarket target that usually forces a regear.

features.name: 17-Inch Off-Road Wheels
features.notes: 17-inch wheels sized for tall off-road tires (builders favor 17s for tire-sidewall options and beadlock fitment). Included with these trims' off-road tire packages.

features.name: Rock Rails
features.notes: Rock-protection sill rails guarding the rockers. Willys via MEF Rock Protection Sill Rails; standard Rubicon equipment. A common bolt-on for Sport / Sport S / Sahara (the Mopar HD Rock Slider, MMU, is a factory accessory across trims).

features.name: Steel Front & Rear Bumpers
features.notes: Steel front and rear bumpers (MC6/MD6). Standard on no trim: a factory option on Rubicon via the Steel Bumper Group (AD3, $1,495 MSRP). The guide's Rubicon/Sahara content pages highlight the steel bumpers, but AD3 is separately priced, so the taxonomy treats them as a factory option, not standard equipment (curator reconciliation). A very common aftermarket swap across all trims.

features.name: Factory Warn Winch
features.notes: Factory-installed Warn winch (XE5, $1,995 MSRP on Rubicon). Requires a steel front bumper (AD3/MC6) and is offered only on Rubicon. Standard on no trim — an option or a common aftermarket recovery add.

features.name: Underbody Skid Plates
features.notes: Transfer-case and fuel-tank skid plates. Standard Rubicon equipment; not line-itemed on the guide's content page, so curator-sourced from Jeep trim spec. Willys carries partial underbody protection; Sport / Sport S / Sahara are common skid-plate add candidates.

features.name: High-Clearance (Highline) Fender Flares
features.notes: High-clearance Rubicon Highline flares for larger-tire clearance. Willys and the base Rubicon use MIC (textured black) highline flares (MMZ); Rubicon can upgrade to body-color (MHV). Aids clearance when running 35s.

features.name: LED Premium Headlamps
features.notes: LED premium reflector headlamps (LPX). Standard on Willys / Rubicon; a $1,695 option (ADA, LED Headlamp & Fog Lamp Group) on Sport S. Base Sport ships with halogens. A frequent early upgrade.

features.name: LED Fog Lamps
features.notes: Front LED fog lamps (LNV). Bundled with the LED headlamps on Willys / Rubicon; optional (ADA) on Sport S.

features.name: LED Taillamps
features.notes: LED taillamps (LAY). Standard on Rubicon; available on Sport S / Willys / Sahara through the Safety Group (AJ1).

features.name: Auxiliary Switches (Factory Upfit Wiring)
features.notes: Dash auxiliary switches (LH2) for wiring lights, a winch, or accessories. Standard on Willys / Rubicon; optional via the Trailer Tow & Aux Switch Group (ADH) on Sport / Sport S / Sahara. Valued by builders for clean accessory wiring.

features.name: 3-Piece Freedom Hard Top
features.notes: Body-color 3-piece Freedom hard top (HT3) standard on Rubicon / Rubicon X. Sport / Sport S / Willys / Sahara ship with a soft top; a black or body-color hard top is a $1,895–$2,195 factory option (HT1/HT3).

features.name: Sunrider Soft Top
features.notes: Standard folding soft top on the non-Rubicon trims. Baseline for the top-choice axis; the Dual Top and Sky One-Touch options replace or supplement it.

features.name: Dual Top Group (Hard + Soft)
features.notes: Factory both-tops option (AEN, $2,495 MSRP): a hard top plus a premium Sunrider soft top. Standard on no trim; mutually exclusive with the Sky One-Touch power top.

features.name: Sky One-Touch Power Top
features.notes: Power canvas roof (STJ, $3,995 MSRP) available on Sport S and up (not base Sport). Standard on no trim. Community risk flag: front-corner / door-gap leaks and hydraulic/electronic stalls are a recurring complaint (discovery/report.md §3.2).

features.name: Uconnect 5 Navigation w/ 12.3" Display
features.notes: 12.3-inch Uconnect 5 with navigation (UBX). Standard on Rubicon / Rubicon X; available on lower trims via the Technology Group (AJY). UConnect 5 is a community reliability watch-item (black-screen / OTA issues, §3.2).

features.name: Alpine Premium Audio
features.notes: Alpine premium speakers and subwoofer (RC4). Standard on Rubicon / Rubicon X and bundled in the Sahara Popular Equipment Group (AYY); a $1,595 option on Sport S / Willys.

features.name: Integrated Off-Road Camera
features.notes: Forward-facing trail camera with washer (XNY). Standard on Rubicon / Rubicon X; part of the Technology Group (AJY) elsewhere.

features.name: Heated Front Seats & Steering Wheel
features.notes: Heated front seats (JPM) and heated steering wheel (NHS). Standard on Rubicon / Rubicon X; bundled in the Convenience Group (AJK) on Sport S / Willys and the Popular Equipment Group (AYY) on Sahara.

features.name: Nappa Leather Seats
features.notes: Nappa leather (VL) standard at no charge on Rubicon X; a $1,995 option on Rubicon (also adds an 8-way power driver seat). Cloth is standard from Sport through Willys.

features.name: Blind Spot & Cross-Path Detection
features.notes: Blind-spot monitoring (XAN). Standard on Rubicon / Rubicon X; available via the Safety Group (AJ1) on Sport S / Willys / Sahara.

features.name: Enhanced Adaptive Cruise Control
features.notes: Adaptive cruise with stop (NH1). Standard from Sport S up (present in Sport S and Willys package content; standard on Rubicon). Base Sport only lacks it. Sahara's base content is not line-itemed on the guide but bundles Sport-S-level ADAS via the Popular Equipment Group (AYY) — curator note.

features.name: Full-Speed Forward Collision Warning Plus
features.notes: Forward collision warning with active braking (LSU). Standard from Sport S up; base Sport only lacks it.

features.name: ParkSense Rear Park Assist
features.notes: Rear parking sensors (XAA). Standard on Rubicon / Rubicon X; via the Safety Group (AJ1) on Sport S / Willys / Sahara.

features.name: Trailer Tow & Hitch Prep
features.notes: Class II receiver hitch, 4-/7-pin wiring, and aux switches (XF2/XFN/LH2, or the AHC Trailer Tow Prep Package on Rubicon). Standard on Willys / Rubicon; a factory option (ADH) on Sport / Sport S / Sahara.
